nativeRange.habitat: Inshore coral reefs in areas with rich coral growth, at depths of 1–5 m

diet.notes: Generally accepts a variety of prepared and frozen marine foods including algae-based flakes, Spirulina, mysis shrimp, and brine shrimp. Sources describe it as herbivore-leaning but it has been observed accepting meaty foods alongside plant-based offerings.

avoidWith: slow-moving fish that may be harassed

summary: A vivid yellow damselfish from the Western Indian Ocean and Red Sea, generally considered hardy and a good candidate for established reef systems with ample rockwork, though tend to be territorial with similar-sized tankmates.
careNotes: The Sulphur Damselfish has been documented inhabiting inshore reef zones at depths of 1–5 m in areas of rich coral growth. It is generally advised to house it in a well-established aquarium of at least 200 L with abundant rockwork providing caves and crevices for territory establishment. Groups of juveniles can coexist but have been known to become increasingly aggressive toward conspecifics and similarly-sized fish as they mature; a larger tank footprint is advisable if multiple individuals are kept. LiveAquaria notes compatibility cautiously in reef settings; isolated instances of fin nipping near corals have been reported. Geographic color variations have been documented across its range. Males guard and aerate demersal, substrate-adhesive eggs during breeding. Temperature notes: LiveAquaria suggests 23.9–27.8°C while FishBase ecological data indicates 26.3–29.2°C; the range used (25–28.5°C) represents an average of both sources. Adult size: LiveAquaria lists 5 in (12.7 cm) while FishBase states a maximum of 11.0 cm TL; range of 7–12 cm used as a reasonable representative span.
breedingNotes: Breeding involves distinct pairing. Eggs are demersal and adhere to the substrate, with males documented guarding and aerating the clutch. Captive breeding has not been widely reported in the aquarium hobby literature reviewed.

primary.name: LiveAquaria
primary.notes: Species page confirmed via collection browse and direct product page. Provided care level, tank size (50 gal), temperature (75–82°F), pH (8.1–8.4), dKH (8–12), diet, temperament, reef safe, adult size (5 in).

additional.name: FishBase
additional.notes: Cross-checked adult size (11.0 cm TL), native range, habitat (1–5 m inshore reef), temperature (26.3–29.2°C), taxonomy (family Pomacentridae), trophic level 2.7, and breeding behavior. Size disagrees slightly from LiveAquaria (11.0 cm vs 12.7 cm); temperature range partially overlaps. Averaged both sources for final values.

fish.breedingNotes: Egg-scattering with demersal adhesive eggs; males guard the clutch. Captive spawning rarely reported in home aquaria.
